region_of_origin: Global (freshwater crayfish widely consumed across North America, Europe, Asia, and Australia)

ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for steamed freshwater crayfish as a food product; SNOMED CT coverage for prepared crustacean dishes is limited.

serving.description: 1 serving (approximately 150 g whole crayfish, yielding ~75 g edible meat)
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of steamed crayfish is approximately 150 g whole weight (roughly 4–6 medium crayfish), yielding about 75 g of edible tail and claw meat after shell removal.

nutrient_source.title: Crayfish, mixed species, cooked, moist heat — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174202); cross-referenced with Singapore HPB Foodo database entry for steamed crayfish (F03050024)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / Health Promotion Board Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Singapore Foodo is the primary source for this record (data_source: hpb, confidence: 0.95). USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for crayfish cooked by moist heat is used as secondary cross-reference. Values are consistent between both sources. HPB values are used as authoritative for this Singapore-registered record.

gi_source.title: N/A — Steamed crayfish contains negligible carbohydrates (0 g/100 g); glycemic index is not applicable
gi_source.source_match_notes: Steamed crayfish contains 0 g carbohydrate per 100 g per HPB Singapore Foodo. Glycemic index is not applicable for foods with negligible carbohydrate content. The numeric value 0 is used here as a sentinel consistent with the template field type (integer); gi_category is set to 'not_applicable' to signal that no GI measurement exists or is meaningful for this food. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium (94 mg/100 g) with high potassium (296 mg/100 g) yielding a favourable Na:K ratio aligned with DASH dietary principles
hypertension.guidance: Steamed crayfish is suitable for individuals with hypertension due to its naturally low sodium content and high potassium; however, patients should avoid soy-based dipping sauces or added salt, which can substantially increase sodium intake and negate this benefit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate and zero sugar content with high protein (16.77 g/100 g) supporting glycaemic stability
type2_diabetes.guidance: Steamed crayfish has no glycaemic impact and is an excellent protein source for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; portion sizes are flexible, but patients should avoid sweet or starchy accompaniments and dipping sauces that would add carbohydrate load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated cholesterol content (133 mg/100 g) relative to lean fish, partially offset by low saturated fat (0.2 g/100 g) and lean overall fat profile (1.2 g/100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Steamed crayfish is low in saturated fat and total fat, making it broadly suitable for cardiovascular health, but the relatively high dietary cholesterol (133 mg/100 g) warrants moderation to portions of 100–150 g for individuals with established cardiovascular disease or hypercholesterolaemia, and butter or cream-based sauces should be avoided.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Absence of significant omega-3 fatty acids or established anti-inflammatory micronutrients limits therapeutic benefit despite low saturated fat and steamed preparation
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Steamed crayfish is not contraindicated in rheumatoid arthritis and its steamed preparation avoids pro-inflammatory advanced glycation end-products (AGEs), but it lacks meaningful omega-3 content and should not be relied upon as an anti-inflammatory food; patients should complement with oily fish rich in EPA and DHA.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Steamed crayfish (also known as steamed crawfish or steamed freshwater crayfish) is a minimally processed crustacean dish prepared by steaming whole freshwater crayfish until the shell turns bright red and the tail meat becomes firm, opaque white, and slightly sweet in flavour. The texture is firm and succulent, similar to a miniature lobster. Nutritionally, steamed crayfish is a high-protein, very low-fat food providing 82 kcal, 16.77 g protein, and only 1.2 g fat per 100 g, with zero carbohydrate and zero sugar, making it suitable for low-carbohydrate, ketogenic, and diabetic dietary patterns. Sodium is low at 94 mg/100 g and potassium is high at 296 mg/100 g, supporting DASH dietary principles for blood pressure management. Cholesterol is relatively elevated at 133 mg/100 g for a shellfish, which warrants moderation for individuals with cardiovascular disease or hypercholesterolaemia. Calcium content of 60 mg/100 g contributes to bone health. Steamed crayfish is consumed globally and is particularly popular in North America (Cajun cuisine), China (Sichuan spiced preparations), Scandinavia, and Australia. In Singapore and Southeast Asia, it is consumed as a seafood dish and is listed in the HPB Foodo database. The steamed preparation is the cleanest nutritional form, avoiding added fats, oils, or sodium from frying or sauce-based cooking methods. Glycemic index is not applicable due to negligible carbohydrate content.
common_user_queries: How many calories in steamed crayfish?
Is steamed crawfish high in cholesterol?
Can I eat crayfish on a keto diet?
Is steamed crayfish low in sodium?
What is the nutritional value of steamed crayfish?

confidence_reason: Primary nutrient data sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo (data_source: hpb, confidence: 0.95, review_status: reviewed), cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for crayfish cooked by moist heat. Values are consistent between both approved sources. Record is marked as canonical and reviewed in the input.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo — steamed freshwater crayfish contains no carbohydrate by composition. Sugar_g is 0.0: naturally zero as crayfish contains no free sugars. Dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: naturally zero as this is an animal-origin food product. Trans_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed negligible/trace per USDA SR Legacy for moist-heat cooked crayfish. Glycemic index field is set to numeric 0 as required by the template integer field type; gi_category is set to 'not_applicable' to signal that no GI measurement is meaningful for this food, which contains 0 g carbohydrate per 100 g per HPB Singapore Foodo; glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 by definition. Saturated_fat_g (0.2 g), monounsaturated_fat_g (0.22 g), and polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.46 g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 174202 (crayfish, mixed species, cooked, moist heat) as HPB does not report fatty acid breakdown; these values are approximate. Fields set to -1 where not reported by HPB Foodo or USDA SR Legacy for this specific preparation: vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g — detailed micronutrient and moisture data not available in HPB Foodo for this record; USDA SR Legacy provides partial micronutrient data for crayfish but not all fields are reported for the steamed preparation. Ontology: FoodOn URI and FoodEx2 code assigned based on best available match for freshwater crayfish food product; SNOMED CT code not assigned as no specific code for steamed freshwater crayfish as a food product was identified. Multilingual local names are not applicable for this generic international food record — no single cuisine origin mandates Chinese, Malay, Tamil, or Hindi local names; the food is not classified as a Singaporean dish. Halal status marked true as freshwater crayfish is generally considered halal under Shafi'i school (dominant in Singapore/Malaysia); consumers should verify with their religious authority. Kosher status false as shellfish are not kosher.
